observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: • Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer Designate to conduct physical count of all property, plant and equipment annually and to submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year in compliance with Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I; • Municipal Accountant to furnish/present the Audit Team with PPE Ledger Cards prepared and maintained by the Accounting Office and the complete and updated Property Cards prepared and maintained by the Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer Designate; and • Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer Designate and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records regularly in order to establish with certainty, the valuation, completeness and existence of the PPE.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer submitted to the Audit Team the RPCPPE for CY 2021.
observations.reason: The Report on the Physical Count of PPE was still not submitted to the Audit Team within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: • require the Municipal Treasurer to pursue the collection of P188,339.25 by sending demand letters to the debtors; • instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to locate the supporting documents to identify the debtor and the preparation of schedule for the amount of P64,238.24 to enforce collection thereof; and • make representation with the Sangguniang Bayan to pass a Resolution authorizing him to file the request for the write-off of the dormant accounts to the Commission on Audit in case actions taken to implement recommendations a and b failed and proved to be futile, by following the procedures prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 16, 2016.
observations.managementAction: No action was undertaken by the Management in CY 2021.
observations.reason: The Loans Receivable-Others account totaling P252,577.49 remained uncollected as of December 31, 2021. The supporting documents to identify the debtors and the schedule for P64,238.24 were still not located. No request for write-off has been filed to the Commission on Audit for the dormant accounts.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to continue the regular reconciliation of their records and adjust any discrepancies that will be noted in order to establish the correct cash account balances in the financial statements; require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with Item 33(a) of the Revised Cash Examination Manual by depositing all collections promptly and intact to avoid possible loss or misuse of funds.
observations.managementAction: No action was undertaken by the Management in CY 2021.
observations.reason: The differences between the two records increased to P8,786.17. Collections were not always deposited intact daily or on the next banking day due to geographical distance to the authorized depository bank, weather abnormalities and availability of passenger vessel, exposing government funds to possible loss or misuse.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team all the required supporting documents and submit written explanation on the noted deficiencies. Otherwise, the same may be suspended or disallowed in audit. Also, ensure that all claims against government funds are properly supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: No action was undertaken by the Management in CY 2021.
observations.reason: Disbursement Vouchers and supporting documents were not submitted to the Audit Team; hence verification could not be performed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team all the required supporting documents and submit written explanation on the noted deficiencies. Otherwise, the same may be suspended or disallowed in audit. Also, ensure that all claims against government funds are properly supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: No action was undertaken by the Management in CY 2021.
observations.reason: Disbursement Vouchers and supporting documents were not submitted to the Audit Team; hence verification could not be performed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• instruct the Municipal Accountant for the immediate submission of the
receipts and disbursement records and all paid vouchers and its
supporting documents, official receipts, reports and supporting
documents for CY 2021;
• instruct the Municipal Accountant and other responsible municipal
officials to submit the required reports within the prescribed period;
• consider withholding the payment of salary of the Municipal Accountant
and other responsible officials until they have complied with the
requirements of the Commission; and
• ensure that the succeeding reports and documents are submitted within
the prescribed period pursuant to existing rules and regulations.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
• Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer Designate to submit the
Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to the
Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year in compliance
with Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
• Municipal Accountant to furnish/present the Audit Team with PPE
Ledger Cards prepared and maintained by the Accounting Office and the
complete and updated Property Cards prepared and maintained by the
Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer Designate; and
• Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer Designate and the
Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records regularly in order to
establish with certainty, the valuation, completeness and existence of
the PPE.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
• Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the concerned offices to seek
or locate the Memorandum of Agreement entered into with the Source
Agencies or any documents stating the purpose of the fund transfer and
the requirements in implementing and reporting of the unutilized PPAs
totaling P6,064,283.87;
• Department heads concerned to secure copies of MOA from the Sourced
Agencies and determine if these projects are still valid for
implementation, otherwise remit the amount to the concerned sourced
agency;
• Municipal Engineer and Municipal Social Welfare and Development
Officer to facilitate the implementation of the specific projects with funds
received from NGAs totaling P5,971,851.86 in accordance with the
provision/s stated in the Memorandum of Agreement; and
• Municipal Treasurer to remit the unexpended fund balance of P92,432.01
to the source agencies in compliance with the Memorandum of
Agreement.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to:
• submit to the Audit Team copies of Journal Entry Vouchers and
necessary supporting documents of the claims listed in the Accounts
Payable totaling P3,153,848.64, if there is any, for our verification;
• ensure that all claims against government funds are properly supported
with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4 (6) of PD No.
1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC Secretariat to
submit to the Audit Team the copies of contracts together with the
supporting documents for the 14 infrastructure projects implemented in CY
2021 and regularly furnished the Audit Team with copies of perfected
contracts together with the supporting documents within five (5) working
days from approval thereof.
Henceforth, instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly adhere and
comply with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated
February 12, 2009 on the prompt submission of perfected contracts and its
supporting documents, to ensure timely review, evaluation and verification
thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer
and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to:
• submit the list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities
(“PPA”) and those that are to be implemented during the year and its
monthly monitoring report within the prescribed period, and
• inform the Audit Team within ten (10) days after the award of the project
or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project
signboards and/or public notices are already posted to facilitate
validation of the same in order to promote transparency, efficiency and
accountability in the use of government resources.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments to reclassify the completed infrastructure projects worth P14,294,734.36 to their respective PPE accounts and the Municipal Engineering Office to continue the regular submission to the Accounting Office of the status of Implementation of Infrastructure Projects for proper recognition in the books of accounts of completed projects and its corresponding depreciation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made adjustments on the reclassification of the 24 infrastructures.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: • instruct the ICO-General Services Officer to maintain Stock Cards for each inventory item, including those that are COVID-19-related, conduct periodic physical count of inventories, and submit the corresponding RPCI complete with necessary information and correct classification of inventory accounts; • require the Municipal Accountant to balance and maintain SLCs for the inventories, record the purchases of supplies/inventories initially under the appropriate asset account, and recognize the expense account once the inventories are consumed/distributed in accordance with IPSAS 12.
observations.managementAction: The Management encouraged the General Services Officer to conduct physical inventory and the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer will reconcile their records and will submit the report to the Audit Team. The Municipality submitted the status and related adjustments.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor require the: • Accountable Officers (AOs) to liquidate their cash advances, otherwise consider deducting the money due them; • Municipal Accountant not to grant additional cash advance to those with outstanding balance and see to it that advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year for fair presentation of financial statements and to allow the granting of succeeding cash advance only when the previous one is fully liquidated; • Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort in retrieving the necessary documents to support the unaccounted cash advances balance of P653,241.66; and • Municipal Accountant to further review the cash advances of those employees who were no longer connected to the Municipality, assess the possibility of requiring them to liquidate their cash advances, and take appropriate action thereon. Henceforth, ensure the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of all cash advances within the prescribed period in accordance with existing rules and regulations and for the fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: A total amount of P94,300.00 was liquidated in CY 2021.
observations.reason: Employees who were granted with cash advances during CYs 2012 to 2016 were no longer connected to the Municipality. Cash advances totaling P5,408,186.01 remained outstanding as of December 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to send to creditors request for confirmation of the amount of P155,826.11. If no replies are received within a reasonable period of time and in the event that they fail to support their claims, revert the amount of P155,826.11 to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund of the Municipality, subject to an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan with a condition that appropriation shall be made available in the event that claim arises in the future, pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reverted the amount of P155,826.11 to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to make a detailed review of the accounts to determine the cause/s of the reflected unreconciled balances and make the appropriate adjustments in the books of accounts to present the correct balances of the affected accounts for their fair presentation in the financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: The unreconciled balances decreased by P8,130,246.16 and P396.70. Due to Other Funds in the amount of P8,023,754.02 for all funds as of December 31, 2021 reflected unreconciled balances of P106,492.14.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GFPS TWG to: • institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation; • undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing gender issues and ensure that the programs, projects and activities are responsive to the said issues; • include in the GAD Plan and Budget the attendance to the seminar and training of the personnel assigned in the GAD as well as the expenses related to development of GAD Database; and • submit/present to the Audit Team proof for status of compliance. Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 8, Part II of this Report.
observations.managementAction: The GAD PB and AR for CY 2020 were submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: GAD PB and AR for CY 2021 were still not submitted to the Audit Team within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to transfer the cash corresponding to the unexpended balance for CY 2020 to the special trust fund account and to record the transfer/adjustments in the Trust Liability-LDRRMF account in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 and for fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements; and require the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the cash corresponding to the unutilized balance of LDRRMF for CY 2020 to the special trust fund account created for the purpose in compliance with the Circular to avoid misapplication of funds and to ensure activities for disaster risk reduction are accomplished.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant transferred the corresponding unexpended balance of P1,408,332.01 to the special trust fund and recorded under the Trust Liability-DRRM account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Disaster and Risk Reduction Management Officer to submit: a) Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances for In-kind Donations; and b) Report on the Receipt and Distribution/Issuance of Donated Relief Goods to the NDRRMC, through the OCD, copy furnished the Audit Team within 10 working days after the end of each quarter pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2014-002 and 2020-009.
observations.managementAction: Reports were furnished to the Audit Team. The OCD furnished the Audit Team with copy of reports within 10 working days after the end of each quarter pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2014-002 and 2020-009.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Environmental and Natural Resources Office and the Solid Waste Management Board to: • develop and operate a sanitary landfill as disposal site for wastes which shall be designed and operated in accordance with the guidelines specified under RA No. 9003; • provide the necessary support in enjoining barangays to establish their own MRFs and to strictly implement waste segregation; • secure approval of the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan from the National Solid Waste Management Commission; and • strictly comply with all other prescribed procedures and requirements under RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality initiated steps to develop and operate a sanitary landfill. A MRF was constructed in Barangay Talisoy. The Municipality had formulated the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan which was still under review and not yet approved by the NSWMC.
observations.reason: The Municipality is still waiting for the response of the Department of Environment and Natural Resources. The 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan was still under review and not yet approved by the NSWMC. Some procedures required under RA 9003 were still not implemented and MRFs were not established in all barangays due to lack of funds.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
• Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer Designate to conduct physical
count of all property, plant and equipment annually and to submit the Report on
the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to the Auditor concerned
not later than January 31 of each year in compliance with Section 124 of the
MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
• Municipal Accountant to furnish/present the Audit Team with PPE Ledger Cards
prepared and maintained by the Accounting Office and the complete and updated
Property Cards prepared and maintained by the Municipal Treasurer/General
Services Officer Designate; and
• Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer Designate and the Municipal
Accountant to reconcile their records regularly in order to establish with certainty,
the valuation and existence of the PPE in accordance with Paragraph 27 of
IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
make the necessary adjustments to reclassify the 24 completed infrastructure
projects worth P14,294,734.36 to their respective PPE accounts and the
Municipal Engineering Office to continue the regular submission to the
Accounting Office of the status of Implementation of Infrastructure Projects for
proper recognition in the books of accounts of completed projects and its
corresponding depreciation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• instruct the ICO-Municipal General Services Officer to maintain Stock Cards
for each inventory item, including those that are COVID-19-related, conduct
periodic physical count of inventories, and submit the corresponding RPCI
to the Audit Team complete with necessary information and correct
classification of inventory accounts; and
• require the Municipal Accountant to maintain SLCs for the inventories,
record the purchases of supplies/inventories initially under the appropriate
asset account, and recognize the expense account once the inventories are
consumed/distributed in accordance with IPSAS 12.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor require the:
• Accountable Officers (AOs) to liquidate their cash advances, otherwise
consider deducting the corresponding amounts from the money due them;
• Municipal Accountant
➢ not to grant additional cash advance to those with outstanding balance
and see to it that advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year for
fair presentation of financial statements and to allow the granting of
succeeding cash advance only when the previous one is fully
liquidated;
➢ to exert extra effort in retrieving the necessary documents to support the
unaccounted cash advances balance of P653,241.66; and
➢ to further review the cash advances of those employees who were no
longer connected to the Municipality, assess the possibility of requiring
them to liquidate their cash advances, and take appropriate action
thereon.

Henceforth, ensure the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of all cash
advances within the prescribed period in accordance with the existing rules and
regulations and for the fair presentation of the accounts in the financial
statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to send to creditors request for confirmation of the amount of P155,826.11.
If no replies are received within a reasonable period of time and in the event that they
fail to support their claims, revert the amount of P155,826.11 to the Unappropriated
Surplus of the General Fund of the Municipality, subject to an authority from the
Sangguniang Bayan with a condition that appropriation shall be made available in the
event that claim arises in the future, pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of PD No.
1445.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to make a detailed review of the accounts to determine the cause/s of the
unreconciled balances and make the appropriate adjustments in the books of accounts
to present the correct balances of the affected accounts for their fair presentation in
the financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• require the Municipal Treasurer to pursue the collection of P188,339.25 by
sending demand letters to the identified debtors;
• instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to locate the supporting
documents to identify the debtor and cause the preparation of schedule for the
amount of P64,238.24 to enforce collection thereof; and
• make representation with the Sangguniang Bayan to pass a Resolution authorizing
him to file the request for the write-off of the dormant accounts to the Commission on
Audit in case actions taken to implement recommendations a and b, above, failed
and proved to be futile, by following the procedures prescribed under COA Circular
No. 2016-005 dated December 16, 2016.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor:
• instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to continue
the regular reconciliation of their records and adjust any discrepancies that will
be noted in order to establish the correct cash account balances in the
financial statements; and
• require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with Item 33(a) of the
Revised Cash Examination Manual by depositing all collections promptly and
intact to avoid possible loss or misuse of funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer Designate and the Municipal Accountant to complete and finish the reconciliation of their records. The Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer Designate to conduct physical count of all property, plant and equipment annually and to submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year in compliance with Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I; Municipal Accountant to furnish/present the Audit Team with PPE Ledger Cards prepared and maintained by the Accounting Office and the complete and updated Property Cards prepared and maintained by the Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer Designate; and Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer Designate and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records regularly in order to establish with certainty, the valuation and existence of the PPE in accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor agreed to the audit recommendations. Also, he commented that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer are going to comply with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of records is on-going. However, RPCPPE was not submitted to the Audit Team within the prescribed period. With the new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew under audit observation no. 1, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: • Accountable Officers (AOs) to liquidate their cash advances, otherwise consider deducting the corresponding amounts from the money due them; • Municipal Accountant not to grant additional cash advance to those with outstanding balance and see to it that advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year for fair presentation of financial statements and to allow the granting of succeeding cash advance only when the previous one is fully liquidated; to exert extra effort in retrieving the necessary documents to support the unaccounted cash advances balance; and to further review the cash advances of those employees who were no longer connected to the Municipality, assess the possibility of requiring them to liquidate their cash advances, and take appropriate action thereon.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor agreed and committed to comply with the recommendations.
observations.reason: There are Cash Advances which remain unliquidated as of December 31, 2020. The Municipal Accountant is still retrieving documents for cash advances granted in the previous years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to make a detailed review of the accounts to determine the cause/s of the unreconciled balances and make the appropriate adjustments in the books of accounts to present the correct balances of the affected accounts for their fair presentation in the financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer started reconciling their records. The Municipal Treasurer’s Office already prepared and maintained Property Cards, while the Municipal Accounting Office already prepared the PPE Ledgers.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of their records is on-going. With the new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew under audit observation no. 6, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: • require the Municipal Treasurer to pursue the collection of P188,339.25 by sending demand letters to the identified debtors; • instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to locate the supporting documents to identify the debtor and cause the preparation of schedule for the amount of P64,238.24 to enforce collection thereof; and • make representation with the Sangguniang Bayan to pass a Resolution authorizing him to file the request for the write-off of the dormant accounts to the Commission on Audit in case actions taken to implement recommendations a and b, above, failed and proved to be futile, by following the procedures prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 16, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor agreed and committed to comply with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer exerted effort to pursue the collection. The Municipal Accountant set-up Allowance for Impairment for Loans Receivable-Others but retrieval of supporting documents was problematic since documents were dated 2010 and most are unaccounted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: • instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to regularly conduct reconciliation of their records and adjust any discrepancies that will be noted in order to establish the correct cash account balances in the financial statements; and • require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with Item 33(a) of the Revised Cash Examination Manual by depositing all collections promptly and intact to avoid possible loss or misuse of funds.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer reconciled their records and made necessary adjustments in the books.
observations.reason: There are blunders/deterrent factors such as navigation schedule, restrictions and quarantine levels brought by COVID-19 pandemic and bad weather condition that affect prompt depositing of collections. Reconciliation is still in process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to further analyze the accounts, exert extra effort in retrieving the documents to confirm with account owners the existence of their claims and process payment if proper. In the event that they fail to support their claims, revert the account to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund of the Municipality, subject to an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan with a condition that appropriation shall be made available in the event that claim arises in the future, pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reverted the balance of Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable of P51,210.94 to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GAD Focal Person to submit to the Audit Team the GAD Plan and Budget and Accomplishment Report within the prescribed period, and ensure submission of complete disbursement vouchers and supporting documents to enable the Audit Team to conduct audit of funds, programs and activities on gender and development.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person submitted the GAD Plan and Budget and Accomplishment Report for CY 2019 to the Audit Team on March 2, 2021 and submitted the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents pertinent thereto. The GAD Focal Person for CY 2020 reorganized and the plan was submitted to DILG but the Audit Team was not furnished; AR for CY 2020 was still for review and finalization.
observations.reason: GAD Plan and Budget and Accomplishment Report for CY 2020 were not submitted within the prescribed period. With new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew under audit observation no. 9, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Office, with the assistance of the Municipal GAD Focal Point System to: institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation; undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing gender issues and ensure that the programs, projects and activities are responsive to the said issues; revisit Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 in the formulation of the Annual GAD Plan; and include in the GAD Plan and Budget the attendance to seminar and training of the personnel assigned in the GAD as well as the expenses related to development of GAD Database.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Point System revisited Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01; institutionalized a GAD database with the assistance of the MLGOO; undertaken gender analysis as reflected in the Annual GAD Plan and Budget; and included attendance to seminar and training and expenses related to development of GAD Database in the GAD Plan and Budget.
observations.reason: GAD Plan and Budget for CY 2020 was not submitted to the Audit Team, hence the same cannot be verified. With new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew under audit observation no. 9, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: • Bids and Awards Secretariat to submit to the Audit Team the copies of perfected contracts together with the supporting documents of the infrastructure projects as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001; • Bids and Awards Committee to strictly adhere and comply with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 on the prompt submission of perfected contracts and its supporting documents, to ensure timely review, evaluation and verification thereof; and • Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the Disbursement Vouchers for CY 2019 for post audit.
observations.managementAction: The Bids and Awards Secretariat submitted to the Audit Team the copies of contracts together with the supporting documents of the 27 infrastructure projects implemented in CY 2019 and regularly furnished the Audit Team with copies of perfected contracts together with the supporting documents. The Bids and Awards Committee complied with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001. The Municipal Accountant submitted to the Audit Team the Disbursement Vouchers for CY 2019 for post audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to submit to the Audit Team the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, in compliance with Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002; and the Accomplishment Report and complete disbursement vouchers for DRRM funds in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-009 dated August 28, 2014 to enable the Audit Team to conduct audit of MDRRM funds.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer submitted the monthly Reports on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF and the Accomplishment Report and disbursement vouchers for DRRM funds to the Audit Team gradually due to geographical location and limited transport service.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Environmental and Natural Resources Office and the Solid Waste Management Board to: • develop and operate a sanitary landfill as disposal site for wastes of the Municipality which shall be designed and operated in accordance with the guidelines specified under RA No. 9003; • provide the necessary support in enjoining the barangays to establish their own MRFs and to strictly implement waste segregation; • secure the approval of its 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan from the National Solid Waste Management Commission; and • strictly comply with all other prescribed procedures and guidelines under RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer submitted formal request to the Department of Environment and Natural Resources (DENR) for technical assistance and support in the preparation of the Proposed Rehabilitation Plan for the development and operation of a sanitary landfill as final disposal site of the Municipality. The Municipality closed the open dumpsite. MENRO reminded barangays on requirement for establishment of their own MRFs and submitted the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan to DENR-EMB for review.
observations.reason: The Municipality is still waiting for response from the DENR regarding the rehabilitation plan. Barangays did not provide funds for MRFs in CY 2020. The 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan is still subject for review of DENR-EMB.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards Secretariat and BAC to comply with COA Circular No. 2009-001 on submission of perfected contracts and supporting documents and require the Municipal Accountant to submit Disbursement Vouchers for post audit.
observations.managementAction: The Bids and Awards Secretariat and BAC submitted the required perfected contracts and supporting documents; the Municipal Accountant submitted the Disbursement Vouchers for post audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant continue reconciliation of the two accounts, identify causes of unreconciled differences, and reflect adjustments in the books.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant continued the reconciliation and reviewed and adjusted balances; reconciliation is in process.
observations.reason: Reconciliation is still ongoing; some differences persisted due to transactions handled by previous accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure implementation of prior year audit recommendations and require submission of Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation (AAPSI) form.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor agreed and committed to comply with the recommendations and submitted some documents and adjustments as applicable.
observations.reason: Some actions are ongoing and certain documents could not be submitted within the prescribed period due to geographical and logistical constraints and the COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer Designate and the Municipal Accountant to complete and finish the reconciliation of their records and any adjustments, if warranted, should be made in order that the existence and valuation of the PPE could be ascertained for the fair presentation of the PPE accounts in the financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
• Accountable Officers (AOs) to liquidate their cash advances, otherwise consider
deducting the corresponding amounts from the money due them;
• Municipal Accountant
➢ not to grant additional cash advance to those with outstanding balance and see
to it that advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year for fair presentation
of financial statements and to allow the granting of succeeding cash advance
only when the previous one is fully liquidated;
➢ to exert extra effort in retrieving the necessary documents to support the
unaccounted cash advances balance of P650,347.16; and
➢ to further review the cash advances of those employees who were no longer
connected to the Municipality, assess the possibility of requiring them to liquidate
their cash advances, and take appropriate action thereon.
Henceforth, ensure the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of all cash advances
within the prescribed period in accordance with the existing rules and regulations and
for the fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements as required under
Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the
Municipal Accountant to make a detailed review of the accounts to determine
the cause/s of the unreconciled balances and make the appropriate adjustments
in the books of accounts to present the correct balances of the affected
accounts for fair presentation in the financial statements as required under
Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
• Municipal Treasurer to pursue the collection of P188,339.25 by sending demand
letters to the identified debtors; and
• Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to locate the supporting documents to
identify the debtor and cause the preparation of schedule for the amount of
P64,238.24 to enforce collection thereof, and if found to be futile, refer to COA
Circular No. 2016-005 for the request for the write-off of dormant accounts.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to set up the provision for Allowance for Impairment in order to
present fairly the Loans Receivable-Others account in the financial statements
at net realizable amount consistent with the requirements of COA Circular No.
2016-005 and for the fair presentation of the account in the financial statements
as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
• Municipal Accountant to transfer the cash corresponding to the unexpended
balance for CYs 2016 and 2019 to special trust fund account and to record the
transfer/adjustments in the Trust Liability-LDRRMF account in compliance to
COA Circular No. 2012-002 and for fair presentation of the accounts in the
financial statements in accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1; and
• Municipal Treasurer to transfer the cash corresponding to the unutilized balance
of the LDRRMF for CYs 2016-2019 to a special trust fund account in compliance
with the same Circular to avoid misapplication of funds and to ensure that
activities for disaster risk reduction and management are accomplished.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant
to send to creditors request for confirmation of the amount of P84,292.50. In the
event that they fail to support their claims, revert the amount totaling
P1,645,814.29 to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund of the
Municipality, subject to an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan with a
condition that appropriation shall be made available in the event that claim
arises in the future, pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of PD No. 1445.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor require the
Municipal Planning and Development Officer and the Municipal Engineer to:
• submit to the Supervising Auditor and the Audit Team Leader at the
beginning of the year, the complete list of all ongoing government
projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during
the year with relevant information as required in Item 2.1 of COA Circular
2013-004;
• inform the Audit Team within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure
project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project
signboards and/or public notices were already posted for prompt validation
and inspection of the Audit Team; and
• regularly submit the monthly monitoring report with supporting documents
in compliance with the existing regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to regularly
conduct reconciliation of their records and adjust any discrepancies that
will be noted in order to establish the correct cash account balances in the
financial statements; and
• require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with Item 33(a) of the
Revised Cash Examination Manual by depositing all collections promptly
and intact to avoid possible loss or misuse of funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant
to further analyze the accounts, exert extra effort in retrieving the documents to
confirm with account owners the existence of their claims from the Municipality,
process payment if proper. In the event that they fail to support their claims,
revert the account to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund of the
Municipality, subject to an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan with a
condition that appropriation shall be made available in the event that claim
arises in the future, pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of PD No. 1445.

15.We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

o instruct the Municipal Accountant for the immediate submission of the
required financial reports, documents and remaining accounts for CY 2018;

« consider withholding the payment of salary of the Municipal Accountant until
she has complied with the requirements of the Commission; and

« ensure that the succeeding reports and documents are submitted within the
prescribed period pursuant to existing rules and regulations.

21.We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:

« MGSO to conduct physical count of all property, plant and equipment and to
'submit the RPCPPE; and

« Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain PPE Ledger Cards and the
MGSO to prepare and maintain complete and updated property cards and
require them to reconcile the records regularly.

28.We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Engineer to submit the list of all on-going government projects/programs/
activities (PPAs) and those that are to be implemented during the year. Likewise,
submit the monthly monitoring report in compliance with the existing regulations,
as well as the disbursement vouchers pertinent thereto.

34.We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GAD
Focal Person to submit to the Audit Team the Accomplishment Report and
pertinent disbursement vouchers for GAD Fund in compliance with COA Circular
No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014, to enable the Audit Team to conduct audit of
funds, programs and activities on gender and development.

40.We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDRRMO
to submit to the Audit Team the Accomplishment Report and complete
disbursement vouchers for DRRM Funds in compliance with COA Circular
No. 2014-009 dated August 28, 2014, to enable the Audit Team to conduct audit of
MDRRM funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
¢ instruct the Municipal Accountant for the immediate submission of

the required financial reports and documents for Calendar Years
2016 and 2017;
s consider withholding the payment of salary of the Municipal
Accountant until she has complied with the requirements of the
Commission; and

o ensure that the succeeding reports and documents are submitted
within the prescribed period pursuant to existing rules and

regulations.

Weiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:

® Municipal Treasurer/GSO to conduct physical count of all property,
plant and equipment and to submit the RPCPPE:

* Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain PPE Ledger Cards
and the MGSO to prepare and maintain complete and updated
property cards and require them to reconcile the records regularly.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the
Municipal Engineer to submit the list of all on-going government
projects/programs/activities (PPAs) and those that are to be implemented during the
year. Likewise, submit the monthly monitoring report in compliance with the
existing regulations, as well as the disbursement vouchers pertinent thereto.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the
GAD Focal Person to submit to the Audit Team the Accomplishment Report and
pertinent disbursement vouchers for GAD Fund in compliance with COA Circular
No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014, to enable the Team to conduct audit of funds,

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the
MDRRMO to submit to the Audit Team the Accomplishment Report and
disbursement vouchers for DRRM Funds in compliance with COA Circular
No. 2014-009 dated August 28, 2014, to enable the Team to conduct audit of
MDRRM funds.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the year-end  Trial Balances and Financial Statements  with supporting schedules, the disbursement  vouchers ~with  supporting documents and revenue accounts for Calendar Years 2014 and 2015 in compliance with Sections 72 and 76, Volume I of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System, to enable the Audit Team to conduct audit to ascertain the balances of the accounts of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant still failed to submit the required Financial Statements and  other financial documents for CY 2016 within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the SCBAA and disclose the reconciliation cither on the face of the SCBAA or in the Notes to Financial Statements in compliance with paragraph 21 of the PPSAS 1 and paragraph 47 of the PPSAS 24, to achieve transparency in financial reporting.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant still failed to submit the SCBAA and to disclose the reconciliation either on the face of the SCBAA or in the Notes to Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MA to prepare BRSs as of December 31, 2015, and reconcile them to the Cash in-Bank balance presented in the Financial ~Statements, henceforth, prepare BRSs on a monthly basis and submit them to the Audit Team for verification within the prescribed period, pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant still failed to prepare and submit to the Audit Team the Bank Reconciliation Statements for all funds as of December 31, 2016

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal ~ Planning and  Development Coordinator to submit the listing of all-on-going government projects/programs/activities and those that are for implementation during the year, as well as information that the appropriate signboards and/or public notices have been already posted in accordance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: The Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator failed to prioritize the implementation of  the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
‘We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

© provide the necessary support in enjoining the barangays to establish their own MRF and to strictly implement waste segregation;

e develop and operate a sanitary landfill as disposal  site for wastes of the Municipality; and

e strictly comply with all other prescribed procedures  and  guidelines  under RA No. 9003.
observations.reason: The Municipality — still failed to comply with the provisions of RA No. 9003 due to failure of the concerned officials and employees to prioritize the recommendation.

observations.managementAction: The amount of Property, Plant and Equipment could not be validated due to non-submission of accounts and  other reports as discussed in paragraphs 8 and 9.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to verify the causes of the remaining abnormal account balances and effect necessary adjusting entries to ensure faimess in the presentation of the Financial Statements.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the current Municipal Accountant to make a detailed review of the accounts to determine the cause/s of the unreconciled balances and provide the necessary adjustment/s in the books of accounts to present the correct balances of the affected accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

e create an Appraisal Committee which will conduct revaluation of PPEs to determine their fair value and estimated remaining useful life of the PPEs pursuant to Paragraph 44 of PPSAS 17; and paragraphs 9 and 10.

e instruct the Municipal Accountant to provide the depreciation using the revalued amount of the above-listed PPEs and compute, record and present the accumulated depreciation in the financial staterments pursuant to Paragraphs 43, 66, 69 and 71 of PPSAS 17 for the proper valuation of PPE and its reporting in the financial statements as well as the accurate reporting of the asset and expense accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to reconcile the accounts and remit whatever balance due to the agencies concerned to avoid incurrence of penalties and surcharges and enable the member beneficiaries of the Municipality to avail the benefits from the said offices.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to analyze and reconcile records pertaining to the incurrence of abnormal balances in the SLs of the Due to BIR, GSIS and Pag-ibig account, initiate immediate adjustments to correct the SL balances of the accounts, and/or refund any over remittances, if any.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Municipal Accountant the certified list of taxpayers showing the amount due and collectible for the year so that the RPT/SET Receivables can be adjusted to reflect the correct balances as of December 31, 2016

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to remit immediately to the Provincial Government of Quezon and to the five barangays the unremitted balance of P372,210.16.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to revert the liability account totaling £1,313,391.00 to the Unappropriated Surplus of the Municipality, pursuant to the provisions of Sections 59 and 98 of P.D. 1445

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Treasurer to strictly comply with the requirements stated under COA  Circular No.92-389 to prevent the possible re-use of those documents for other claims of same naure as part of a sound internal control system in the disbursement of funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer provide the Audit Team with any evidence that the Philippine Coast Guard suspended sea travel on December 1-10, 2014 due to inclement weather condition. Otherwise, instruct the concemed employees to immediately refund the amount of P3361.02.

Henceforth, require the Municipal Treasurer to remit the GSIS contributions/premiums in compliance with the aforecited regulations

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer see to it that all the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with insurance with the GSIS in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 and Republic Act 656, thus leaving the properties at risk of total loss without the right of indemnification.
observations.managementAction: The municipal ambulance  and service  vehicles were insured with the  Government Service Insurance System (GSIS).
observations.reason: Other properties of the Municipality still remain uninsured due to lack of funds.
